title: Schema handling during install
body:
When a vendor repo ships a templates/config.schema file, the framework should install it to .vendored/manifests/<vendor>.schema alongside existing manifest files (.files, .version, .deps). The framework handles placement -- vendors do not need a new env var or explicit install logic. Same pattern as .version and .files: the framework reads from the vendor source and writes to the right location.

## Acceptance Criteria

- During install, if templates/config.schema exists in the vendor repo, copy it to .vendored/manifests/<vendor>.schema
- If no schema exists, skip silently (backwards compatible)
- Schema is overwritten on update (framework-managed, not user-editable)

## Implementation Notes

- Download logic should follow the existing pattern used for deps.json -- try to fetch from vendor repo at the resolved ref, handle 404 gracefully
- Place the download in download_and_run_install() or as a post-install step alongside manifest writing
- Add .schema to the list of manifest files that remove cleans up

## Testing

- Test install with a vendor that has templates/config.schema -- verify file appears in manifests/
- Test install with a vendor that does NOT have templates/config.schema -- verify no error, no file
- Test update -- verify schema is overwritten with new version
- Test remove -- verify .schema file is cleaned up

## Docs

- Update docs/vendor-install-dir-guide.md or README to document the schema file convention

title: Audit command for config validation
body:
New command: .vendored/audit -- validates consumer configs against installed schemas. Separate from check. check = file protection (binary pass/fail, every PR). audit = config validation (advisory warnings, on-demand).

## Checks Performed

1. Required fields -- every required field in the schema is present in the config
2. Unknown fields -- config has fields not in any installed vendor schema
3. Cross-vendor misplacement -- field X exists in vendor A config but matches vendor B schema
4. Type checking -- basic type validation (string, array, object, number, boolean)

## Output Format

.vendored/audit should produce human-readable output:

  pearls.json
    [PASS] prefix: required field present
    [PASS] epics: valid optional field

  madreperla.json
    [PASS] description: required field present
    [WARN] provider: unknown field (not in madreperla schema)

  Summary: 1 warning, 0 errors

## Acceptance Criteria

- .vendored/audit reads all .vendored/manifests/*.schema files
- Cross-references against all .vendored/configs/*.json files
- Reports required-missing as errors, unknown/misplaced as warnings
- Exit code 0 on warnings-only, non-zero on errors (missing required fields)
- Ignores _vendor key in configs (framework metadata, not a user field) -- or .registry after migration

## Schema Format

Minimal JSON -- field names, required/optional, basic types:

  {
    "vendor": "<vendor-name>",
    "fields": {
      "field_name": {
        "required": true,
        "type": "string",
        "description": "Human-readable description"
      }
    }
  }

Top-level fields only. No nested field validation. Types: string, number, boolean, array, object.

## Implementation Notes

- New Python script at templates/audit (installed to .vendored/audit)
- Follow the same config loading pattern as check and feedback scripts
- Add to install.sh bootstrap list so it gets installed with the framework

## Testing

- Test with valid configs matching schemas -- all PASS
- Test with missing required field -- error reported, non-zero exit
- Test with unknown field -- warning reported, zero exit
- Test with cross-vendor misplaced field -- warning reported
- Test with type mismatch -- error or warning reported
- Test with no schemas installed -- graceful exit, no errors
- Test that _vendor key is ignored

## Docs

- Add audit command to README command reference
- Document schema format for vendor authors

title: Registry migration (_vendor to manifests/.registry)
body:
Move framework metadata from inside config files (the _vendor key) into dedicated .vendored/manifests/<vendor>.registry files. This separates framework-managed metadata from user-owned config.

## Current State

Today _vendor lives inside each config:

  {
    "_vendor": { "repo": "...", "install_branch": "...", "protected": [...] },
    "prefix": "prl"
  }

## Target State

After migration, _vendor content moves to .vendored/manifests/<vendor>.registry:

  {
    "repo": "mangimangi/pearls",
    "install_branch": "chore/install-pearls",
    "protected": [".pearls/**"],
    "allowed": [".pearls/issues.jsonl"]
  }

And the config becomes purely user-owned:

  {
    "prefix": "prl"
  }

## Acceptance Criteria

- install writes .vendored/manifests/<vendor>.registry from _vendor data
- check reads from .registry files, falls back to _vendor in config for backwards compatibility
- install strips _vendor from configs on update
- Existing consumer repos with _vendor in configs continue to work (fallback)

## Implementation Notes

- Affects templates/install (write .registry, strip _vendor from config)
- Affects templates/check (read from .registry, fallback to config _vendor)
- Affects templates/remove (clean up .registry file)
- Affects templates/feedback (read repo/support info from .registry)
- The config loading helpers (_extract_vendor_registry, load_config) need to support both sources
- Migration should be idempotent -- running install multiple times should converge

## Testing

- Test install writes .registry file with correct content
- Test install strips _vendor from config file
- Test check reads from .registry when present
- Test check falls back to _vendor in config when no .registry exists
- Test remove cleans up .registry file
- Test feedback reads from .registry
- Test idempotency -- install twice produces same result

## Docs

- Document the migration in README or a migration guide
- Update vendor-install-dir-guide.md to reflect new manifest structure
